summary.intent: Fix issue #788 where Telegram users couldn't use /stop or /peek commands by creating a centralized command registry in moltis-channels so all channels derive their command lists from a single source of truth.
summary.outcome: Implemented the full centralized command registry (crates/channels/src/commands.rs), wired all 6 channels to use it, added a gateway coverage test, fixed pre-existing clippy issues, resolved PR review comments, merged main, and landed PR #794 with a 5/5 Greptile confidence score.

repo: Each channel had its own hardcoded command list that had drifted: Telegram was missing stop/peek, Slack was missing 8 commands, Nostr had no command interception at all.
The /sh command is a special case: it only intercepts toggle sub-commands (empty, 'on', 'off', 'exit', 'status'), not arbitrary shell input — this required custom logic in is_channel_command().
WhatsApp and MS Teams already route all / prefixes to gateway and needed no changes.
Matrix was intercepting ALL /-prefixed messages without calling is_channel_command(), causing unknown commands and /sh arbitrary input to error instead of reaching the LLM.
moltis-gateway depends on moltis-channels, so the new registry was accessible for the gateway coverage test without adding a new dependency.
moltis-skills and moltis-node-host had pre-existing clippy failures on main that needed to be fixed before the PR could pass lint.

code.finding: New centralized registry: CommandDef struct, all_commands() slice, is_channel_command() with /sh special-case logic, and help_text() generator. 16 commands across session/control/meta categories.

Nostr had zero command interception before this change. Pattern added: check is_channel_command() before dispatch_to_chat, handle /help locally, dispatch others via dispatch_command(), send response via gift wrap DM — same pattern as WhatsApp (crates/whatsapp/src/handlers.rs:396-424).
Was intercepting all /-prefixed messages unconditionally. Fixed to call is_channel_command() first so unknown commands and /sh arbitrary input fall through to the LLM.
Tests imported the HELP_TEXT constant that was removed and replaced with a help_text() function call — required updating the test imports.
Discord name validation regex was [a-z_] but Discord's actual requirement is [a-z0-9_-]. Test was overly restrictive.
Added bidirectional coverage test asserting every command in all_commands() (except 'help') has a matching arm in dispatch_command — catches future registry/dispatch drift at test time.
tsc_cmd ran npx tsc --noEmit without ensuring node_modules existed first. Fixed with same guard pattern used by e2e commands: if [ ! -d node_modules ]; then npm ci; fi.

workflow: Pre-existing lint failures in unrelated crates (moltis-skills, moltis-node-host) blocked just lint even when the changed crates were clean — need to run clippy scoped to affected crates to validate own changes.
Greptile automated review caught two real bugs: Matrix unconditional interception (P1) and Discord regex mismatch (P2) — both fixed before merge.
Merge conflicts after merging main were both identical clippy fixes applied independently on both branches — resolved by taking main's version.
The npx tsc 'not the tsc command you are looking for' error is a Yarn/npm shim that fires when TypeScript isn't in node_modules — needs npm ci guard before invoking tsc.

friction: Pre-existing clippy failures in moltis-skills and moltis-node-host blocked full lint runs and required fixing unrelated code before the PR could pass CI.
Matrix tests imported the HELP_TEXT constant that was removed mid-implementation, causing a compile failure discovered during the commit/push flow rather than during initial coding.
Greptile check output contained control characters that broke jq parsing, requiring a Python fallback to read the status.
open_items: Discord slash commands for commands that require arguments (e.g., /approve N) — Discord doesn't support freeform args on slash commands by default; these currently work via text-based / input only.
Centralized /help handler in the gateway — currently each channel handles help locally, which means help text could still drift if a channel overrides it.
Fallback models and markdown editing from web UI (separate features mentioned in issue #788 context but explicitly out of scope).
